column3: Garlic's ability to boosts immunity refers to its potency in aiding your body in combating illnesses and promoting overall health by strengthening the immune system.
Garlic's ability to lower blood pressure implies that this plant can help regulate and reduce high blood pressure levels, leading to an improvement in overall cardiovascular health.
Garlic's ability to reduce cholesterol refers to its role in lowering bad cholesterol levels and maintaining a healthy balance in the body.
Garlic's ability to prevent blood clotting refers to its properties that aid in maintaining optimal circulation, ensuring blood flows smoothly without forming harmful clots.
Garlic's ability to fight inflammation refers to its capacity in reducing or managing bodily swelling and discomfort caused by irritants.
Garlic's ability to protect against oxidation refers to its antioxidant properties that help prevent cell damage caused by free radicals.
Garlic's ability to support digestive health refers to its beneficial effect on digestion and overall gut health.
Garlic's ability to manage blood sugar levels refers to its potential to help regulate glucose levels in the body, leading to healthier blood sugar balance.
Garlic's ability to exhibit antimicrobial properties refers to its capability of fighting against harmful microorganisms, like bacteria and fungi, thus providing protection and preservation.

Garlic's ability to prevent cancer refers to its compound called allicin, which might have anti-carcinogenic properties that help reduce the risk of developing certain types of cancer when included in your regular diet.
